methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Baykar is a private-stage defense and security company founded by Özdemir Bayraktar. The valuation universe comprises 10 total comparable companies: 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. While 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier datapoints yielded fewer than three viable comparables—the minimum threshold required for robust median analysis. A fallback to all-sector median pricing would introduce material distortion given Baykar's specialized position within the defense ecosystem and the heterogeneity of business models, geographies, and technological focus across broader Defense & Security.

The analysis cannot triangulate to a supported valuation range without additional methodological inputs (precedent transaction multiples, DCF assumptions, or expanded comparable data across stage and geography). Key uncertainties include the absence of disclosed capital raises or current valuation, which prevents direct benchmarking against peer funding rounds or valuations. Resolution of this valuation would require either expansion of the comparable set to include same-stage defense technology peers across geographies, or application of transaction-based or cash-flow methodologies with disclosed financial data.
